approach: Fisher info / Stam / de Bruijn family — direction analysis + numerical scatter
verdict_short: Fisher info family bounds ||f*f||_inf from ABOVE (Hardy-CS, Stam, infty-Renyi-EPI), not below; convex {I<=K} constraint shrinks feasible set giving UB not LB; SDP+Fisher cap was non-binding at d=4,6,8 (delta=0); direction DEAD for improving 1.2802.
verdict_long: Probed four classical Fisher / EPI inequalities applied to f >= 0 supp f in [-1/4,1/4], int f = 1.  (i) Hardy-CS: ||g||_inf <= 1/(2L) + sqrt(I(g)) gives an UPPER bound on M.  (ii) Stam: 1/I(f*g) >= 1/I(f) + 1/I(g) gives I(f*f) <= I(f)/2, again UPPER on the convolution Fisher info.  (iii) infty-Renyi-EPI (Rogozin): ||f*f||_inf <= ||f||_inf/sqrt(2), UPPER. (iv) de Bruijn identity: dh(f*phi_t)/dt = I(f*phi_t)/2, just a flow identity.  All four give wrong direction.  Convex {I(f) <= K} can be added to SDP relaxation but restricts feasible set, yielding an UB on C_{1a} not LB; non-convex {I(f) >= K} cannot be encoded.  Numerically: at d=4,6,8 the Fisher cap K = 1.5 * I(arcsine) was completely non-binding (LB unchanged to 5+ digits).  Random feasible scatter at d=16 gave Spearman rho(I, M) = 0.348 (positive but weak): high Fisher info correlates with high M, which is the opposite of what would yield a lower-bound mechanism (we'd need: low M => high I, which is false -- uniform gives low I=8 and low M=2).  Verdict: Fisher information is structurally incompatible with lower-bounding ||f*f||_inf via convex relaxation; no mechanism in the family produces a direction-correct, monotone bound.  DIRECTION DEAD.  Note: the same conclusion was reached for entropy in delsarte_dual/ideas_entropy.md; Fisher info is a strict refinement of differential entropy (h' = I/2) and inherits the wrong direction.
